title: Fix #12089: forward actual quality param to GPT Image provider
author: ZevrinBuilds
pr_body_excerpt:
**_Fixes #12089_**

### _Problem_

> In `gen.pollinations.ai/src/image/createAndReturnImages.ts`, the quality parameter for the GPT Image handler was clamped using:
> `const quality = safeParams.quality === "high" ? "high" : "medium";`

> This meant any quality other than "high" (including "low" and "hd") was remitted to "medium". Consequently, users selecting "low" quality were still charged at the "medium" rate, and "hd" quality was silently degraded.

### _Solution_

> Replaced the clamping logic with:
> `const quality = safeParams.quality === "hd" ? "high" : safeParams.quality;`
> 
> This allows "low", "medium", and "high" to pass through to the provider unchanged (ensuring correct billing), while safely mapping "hd" to "high" (since OpenAI/Azure do not accept a native "hd" quality value).

gist.headline: Quality control restored
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where image quality settings were getting stuck in the middle. Now, 'low' quality actually saves you Pollen, and 'hd' requests give you the crisp pixels you asked for!
gist.summary: Fixed a parameter clamping bug in the GPT Image provider to ensure 'low', 'medium', and 'high' quality settings pass through correctly, while mapping 'hd' to 'high'.
gist.impact: Users requesting 'low' quality images will now be billed at the correct lower rate instead of the 'medium' rate, and 'hd' requests will no longer be silently degraded.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee adjusting a retro dial or slider on a machine with distinct settings for LOW, MED, and HIGH. The Monitor Robot is nearby, displaying a crisp, high-definition image on its screen to represent the restored HD quality.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot adjusting a large retro dial on a glowing machine with settings for 'LOW', 'MED', and 'HIGH'. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head gives a thumbs up, its screen displaying a crisp, high-definition pixel flower. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
